# Taxonomy Query Patterns

Use these patterns to expand a user's taxon into practical literature-harvest queries.

## New Taxa

```text
<taxon> sp. nov.
<taxon> spp. nov.
<taxon> gen. nov.
<taxon> new species
<taxon> new genus
<taxon> new taxon
<taxon> taxonomic novelty
```

## Revisions And Treatments

```text
<taxon> taxonomy
<taxon> taxonomic revision
<taxon> revision
<taxon> review of
<taxon> description of
<taxon> redescription
<taxon> key to species
<taxon> checklist
```

## Animal Taxonomy

Useful broad pivots:

```text
Diptera sp. nov.
Diptera new species
Diptera taxonomy Zootaxa
Diptera taxonomy ZooKeys
Coleoptera sp. nov.
Hymenoptera sp. nov.
Lepidoptera sp. nov.
```

## Plant Taxonomy

Useful broad pivots:

```text
<family> sp. nov.
<family> new species
<family> taxonomic revision
<genus> sp. nov.
<genus> new species
```

Add botanical journals or repositories when needed:

```text
PhytoKeys <taxon> new species
Phytotaxa <taxon> new species
Kew Bulletin <taxon> new species
```

## High-Yield Taxonomy Journals

Add journal names to focus downloads:

```text
Zootaxa <taxon> new species
ZooKeys <taxon> new species
European Journal of Taxonomy <taxon> new species
Biodiversity Data Journal <taxon> new species
PhytoKeys <taxon> new species
Phytotaxa <taxon> new species
```

## Notes

- Avoid plain `fruit fly` unless the user wants both Tephritidae and Drosophilidae.
- For true fruit flies, prefer `Tephritidae`.
- For vinegar/fruit flies, prefer `Drosophilidae` or `Drosophila`.
- For broad animal taxonomy, order-level queries often produce much more material than family-level queries.
